question_text: Two bar magnets are placed with their north poles facing each other, 5 cm apart. The force of repulsion between them is 0.8 N. If the magnets are moved so they are 10 cm apart, what will happen to the repulsive force?
correct_answer: The force will decrease to about 0.2 N (or one-quarter of the original force). When the distance doubles, the force becomes one-quarter because magnetic force follows an inverse square law: if distance doubles (×2), force becomes 1/(2²) = 1/4 of the original.
explanation: Magnetic force decreases with the square of the distance. When distance doubles from 5 cm to 10 cm, the force reduces by a factor of 2² = 4, so 0.8 N ÷ 4 = 0.2 N. This is why magnets are much weaker when farther apart.
